item.title: Stream developer agent output to terminal in real-time
item.intent: During a sprint, the developer agent's output streams to the terminal live — users can see what the agent is writing rather than watching a spinner for 5-10 minutes with no feedback.
item.description: The current polling loop shows 'Waiting for agent...' with no visibility into what the developer agent is doing. For a 5-10 minute sprint, users are completely blind. run_agent_process() writes agent output to a temp log; reading that log live (via tail -f or a poll loop printing new lines) gives real-time feedback with zero changes to agent behavior. This is the 'agent output streaming' item from the roadmap Next section.
steps: In run_agent_process(), capture claude output to a temp log file (already done for result.json detection); read and print new lines from that log in the poll loop alongside the 'waiting' status
Rate-limit output printing to avoid overwhelming the terminal — print at most N new lines per poll interval (e.g., every 2 seconds)
Add streaming.enabled config key (default: true) and AISHORE_STREAMING env var override
When streaming.enabled is false, fall back to current 'Waiting for agent...' behavior
Streaming must not interfere with result.json detection or timeout behavior
acceptanceCriteria: During a sprint, developer agent output lines appear in the terminal as the agent writes them
Sprint orchestration (validation, commit, merge) still works correctly with streaming enabled
AISHORE_STREAMING=false disables streaming and reverts to spinner behavior
Timeout still fires correctly when streaming is enabled
